subject: Allow KuD installer to specify Kubespray 2.14.1 or 2.16.0
commit_message:
Allow KuD installer to specify Kubespray 2.14.1 or 2.16.0

The default is 2.14.1 (the existing value).  Setting
KUBESPRAY_VERSION=2.16.0 in the installer environment uses the newer
Kubespray version.

The newer Kubespray version installs Kubernetes 1.20.7.  Kubernetes
1.20.7 comes with following caveats:
- The Virtlet addon is disabled; it does not work with 1.20.7.  This
  requires removing the plugin_fw test as well.
- Kubernetes 1.20.7 removed support for basic auth.

Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-1251
Signed-off-by: Todd Malsbary <todd.malsbary@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic8b9fb1f3effc31da58de5bb3768ed9e509d50de

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I made a pass at using k8s 1.19.3.  The main issue I've noticed is that virtlet does not work: it causes an issue that I haven't been able to track down that results in the kubelet becoming NotReady (Kubelet stopped posting node status).  I updated the virtlet version to 1.5.1 with no improvement; the criproxy version is already at the latest.

Given the age of virtlet and what k8s versions it claims to support, this does not appear too surprising to me.

Patch Set 3:

This will have side effect on vFW CNF use case as it still uses virtlet. It means that we need KUD version that will work with this use case

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> This will have side effect on vFW CNF use case as it still uses virtlet. It means that we need KUD version that will work with this use case

Thanks Lukasz, that's what I needed to know.  I'll rework this to support both versions.

Latest patchset defaults to existing version unless KUBESPRAY_VERSION=2.16.0 is set in the environment prior to running the installer.
